chunk_summary: This section discusses intergenerational impacts of maternal and adolescent nutrition on pediatric outcomes, emphasizing program adherence, risk assessment, and preventive strategies for improving child growth and maternal health.
learning_objectives: Understand the role of maternal and adolescent nutrition in pediatric outcomes.
Learn how to document maternal program exposure during infant visits.
Identify risk factors linked to non-adherence to IFA and calcium supplementation.
Explore preventive strategies for adolescent girls to improve future pregnancy outcomes.
key_facts: Maternal anaemia and low dietary calcium intake increase risks of low birthweight and prematurity.
Non-adherence to IFA and calcium supplementation during pregnancy/postpartum is a child risk factor.
National anaemia strategy and POSHAN 2.0 adopt a life-cycle approach to nutrition.
Adolescent nutrition platforms like RKSK and POSHAN 2.0 include weekly IFA and deworming convergence.
Saksham Anganwadi and POSHAN 2.0 track adolescent girls for nutrition services.
reference_quote: National anaemia strategy and POSHAN 2.0 converge on a life-cycle approach, so clinic documentation should connect maternal and adolescent programme exposure to infant risk assessment.
document_key_topics: Maternal nutrition and its impact on child health
